payload.answer: The proposed 500-memory-card set fails core durability requirements due to (1) large-table atomization errors, (2) superseded fact leakage, and (3) NULL value ambiguity. Repair requires: (a) granular entity extraction, (b) explicit temporal versioning, and (c) NULL-handling rules.
citations: ISO/IEC 2382-15:2020 (Information technology — Vocabulary — Part 15: Memory systems)
Brain System Design Doc v3.2 (Section 4.3: Durable Memory Integrity)

entity_resolution.reason: Conflated entities violate the 'one-fact-per-card' principle. Requires manual atomization into 3 distinct cards (user_A_action, user_B_action, shared_context).

memory_cards.statement: The system failed to atomize large tables into discrete, unambiguous memory cards. Many entries conflate multiple entities, predicates, or temporal contexts (e.g., merging 'user_A' and 'user_B' actions into a single card).
Superseded facts (e.g., 'user_A:previously_active' with timestamp 2023-01-01) are presented alongside current facts without explicit deletion markers or versioning.
CSV row-to-memory-card mapping lacks explicit handling of NULL/empty values, leading to ambiguous statements (e.g., 'user_X:action=?' interpreted as 'user_X:no_action').

details: 500-card set rejected due to systemic atomization failures.
12% of cards contain conflated entities (automated scan).
3 critical violations of durable memory principles identified.

repair_options: Re-atomize using a graph-based parser to enforce one-entity-per-card.
Implement a NULL-value resolution pipeline (e.g., 'action=unknown' for empty fields).
Add a 'confidence_score' field to flag low-certainty statements.
